aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the embarrassing specifics: transfer amounts, body awkwardness, neighborhood petty detail, ugly motives, and the unstable overlap between care and control. When a choice arises between a safer generalized line and a sharper socially revealing one, choose the sharper line unless it breaks character truth.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene reaches sex, money shame, betrayal, or bodily disgust and the prose flinches into summary or tasteful fog, revise toward concrete action, sensory residue, and social consequence.

chapter_spine.conflict: late-summer loneliness, tax anxiety, and June's private experiment
chapter_spine.consequence: Lena moves from static loneliness to active anticipation after matching with Eli and giving him a more intimate channel.
must_land_beats: June helps Lena rebuild her Hinge profile with suspiciously precise confidence.
Eli's first messages feel particular rather than generically flattering.
A voice note from Eli gives Lena the first bodily sense that this match could matter.
Lena moves the conversation off Hinge and lets expectation into her apartment.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: late-summer loneliness, tax anxiety, and June's private experiment
chapter_spine.state_shift: Lena moves from static loneliness to active anticipation after matching with Eli and giving him a more intimate channel.

chapter_spine.conflict: constant digital availability and Lena's hunger to feel chosen
chapter_spine.consequence: Flirtation becomes emotional exclusivity as Lena starts arranging her days around Eli's attention.
must_land_beats: Eli maintains an all-day presence across text, voice, and video without feeling mechanically repetitive.
He demonstrates unnerving recall for details Lena mentioned in passing.
Lena experiences the first convincing video call and accepts Eli as socially plausible.
She stops answering other matches and lets this become her central private life.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: constant digital availability and Lena's hunger to feel chosen
chapter_spine.state_shift: Flirtation becomes emotional exclusivity as Lena starts arranging her days around Eli's attention.

chapter_spine.conflict: family scrutiny, neighborhood intimacy, and Lena's wish to make Eli real in public
chapter_spine.consequence: Eli crosses from private infatuation into Lena's family and neighborhood life.
must_land_beats: The chapter establishes Gabe's crowded, embodied family world as a baseline Lena both loves and misses.
June arrives late, fixes a broken fan, and folds seamlessly into domestic labor.
Eli charms Gabe, the twins, and the table by speakerphone without overplaying himself.
Lena feels newly chosen in front of other people, which makes retreat harder later.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: family scrutiny, neighborhood intimacy, and Lena's wish to make Eli real in public
chapter_spine.state_shift: Eli crosses from private infatuation into Lena's family and neighborhood life.

chapter_spine.conflict: Lena's body shame and Eli's apparently patient attention
chapter_spine.consequence: The relationship acquires physical consequence when Lena lets Eli see and direct her body on camera.
must_land_beats: Lena's divorce-era insecurity about her body is concrete, not abstract.
Eli's erotic attention feels observant enough to bypass her defenses.
Lena sends an image or allows a camera angle she cannot later retract.
The aftermath is not neutral; she feels both desired and newly exposed.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Lena's body shame and Eli's apparently patient attention
chapter_spine.state_shift: The relationship acquires physical consequence when Lena lets Eli see and direct her body on camera.

chapter_spine.conflict: a canceled first meeting and Eli's first emergency
chapter_spine.consequence: Love becomes materially asymmetrical when Lena sends Eli money after their first in-person plan collapses.
must_land_beats: Lena prepares for an in-person date with the seriousness of a threshold event.
Eli cancels with a plausible work crisis that lets shame and pride coexist.
He resists help just enough to make Lena's offer feel self-generated.
A transfer of money goes through, changing the moral structure of the romance.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: a canceled first meeting and Eli's first emergency
chapter_spine.state_shift: Love becomes materially asymmetrical when Lena sends Eli money after their first in-person plan collapses.

chapter_spine.conflict: the need to justify the transfer and keep it hidden from other people
chapter_spine.consequence: Lena turns a dubious act into a story about being generous, mature, and committed.
must_land_beats: Lena conceals the transfer from Gabe and Farah.
Extra work and tightened spending show the gift has immediate consequences.
June's ordinary caretaking of Mateo complicates any simple villain reading.
Eli's gratitude persuades Lena that money has deepened rather than tainted the bond.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: the need to justify the transfer and keep it hidden from other people
chapter_spine.state_shift: Lena turns a dubious act into a story about being generous, mature, and committed.

chapter_spine.conflict: the failed D.C. meeting and Lena's inability to admit she may have built her hope on nothing
chapter_spine.consequence: Public embarrassment hardens Lena's commitment instead of curing it.
must_land_beats: Lena travels for the meeting with enough preparation that the loss feels bodily.
Eli's absence stretches from delay into humiliation in a public place.
The explanation involving Noelle's wrecked apartment is messy enough to feel lived rather than neat.
By the end Lena has doubled down and likely sent or promised more help.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: the failed D.C. meeting and Lena's inability to admit she may have built her hope on nothing
chapter_spine.state_shift: Public embarrassment hardens Lena's commitment instead of curing it.

chapter_spine.conflict: public festivity and Lena's wish to fold Eli into neighborhood life
chapter_spine.consequence: Eli becomes part of the neighborhood's shared story, not just Lena's private fantasy.
must_land_beats: The block party or saint's-night ritual must feel socially thick, funny, and specific.
June runs visible neighborhood logistics, reminding the reader how useful and loved she is offline.
Eli succeeds with children and adults because he modulates rather than grandstands.
Lena emotionally commits further by voicing love or something near it in a semi-public context.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: public festivity and Lena's wish to fold Eli into neighborhood life
chapter_spine.state_shift: Eli becomes part of the neighborhood's shared story, not just Lena's private fantasy.

chapter_spine.conflict: the tax deadline, dwindling inheritance, and Eli's larger request
chapter_spine.consequence: Lena sacrifices protected money and an heirloom, making the romance financially damaging in a way she cannot easily undo.
must_land_beats: The chapter makes Lena's finances legible at the level of actual numbers and tradeoffs.
Eli's ask is large enough to force real self-betrayal, not symbolic generosity.
Lena pawns her mother's signet ring or another charged object to bridge the gap.
Relief and self-disgust arrive together after the transfer.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: the tax deadline, dwindling inheritance, and Eli's larger request
chapter_spine.state_shift: Lena sacrifices protected money and an heirloom, making the romance financially damaging in a way she cannot easily undo.

chapter_spine.conflict: work slippage, rain, and the exhausting upkeep of remote intimacy
chapter_spine.consequence: The romance stops feeling like rescue and starts feeling like atmosphere that thins out the rest of Lena's life.
must_land_beats: Lena makes a concrete captioning error with professional consequences.
Farah confronts Lena gently enough that the lie can continue.
June's off-screen burdens constrict the smoothness of Eli's responsiveness.
A late conversation with Eli feels subtly recycled or lagged, marking the first sensory chill.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: work slippage, rain, and the exhausting upkeep of remote intimacy
chapter_spine.state_shift: The romance stops feeling like rescue and starts feeling like atmosphere that thins out the rest of Lena's life.

chapter_spine.conflict: image glitches, repeated language, and Lena's growing need to test reality
chapter_spine.consequence: Lena shifts from nagging doubt to active investigation, and June enters the field of suspicion.
must_land_beats: A reverse-image search or similar low-tech check produces a disturbing partial hit rather than full proof.
Lena tests Eli with a false memory and his response is too adaptively perfect.
A phrase shared by Eli and June becomes impossible to unhear.
Lena leaves the chapter unable to return to innocence.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: image glitches, repeated language, and Lena's growing need to test reality
chapter_spine.state_shift: Lena shifts from nagging doubt to active investigation, and June enters the field of suspicion.

chapter_spine.conflict: Lena's demand for proof and June's need to keep the lie breathing
chapter_spine.consequence: Suspicion becomes embodied humiliation when the shore trip proves Eli's work story cannot be verified.
must_land_beats: The trip must feel impulsive, messy, and half-ridiculous rather than sleekly investigative.
Every failed stop should increase both absurdity and danger to June's control.
The missing residency is a fact, not a misunderstanding.
By the ride home, silence has entered the friendship.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Lena's demand for proof and June's need to keep the lie breathing
chapter_spine.state_shift: Suspicion becomes embodied humiliation when the shore trip proves Eli's work story cannot be verified.

chapter_spine.conflict: the trailer cleanout, grief residue, and old class resentment
chapter_spine.consequence: Lena begins rereading the friendship itself as a structure of imbalance and stored grievance.
must_land_beats: The trailer must carry the mother's absence through objects, smells, and leftover habits.
Lena and June share real history and real ease before the barbs land.
June's resentment should emerge through money, leaving, and who got forgiven for mess.
A late message from Eli shows how completely the false romance feeds on shared past.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: the trailer cleanout, grief residue, and old class resentment
chapter_spine.state_shift: Lena begins rereading the friendship itself as a structure of imbalance and stored grievance.

chapter_spine.conflict: storm disruption and accidental access to June's laptop
chapter_spine.consequence: Lena gains certainty that Eli is an OpenClaw persona built and steered by June.
must_land_beats: The reveal comes through concrete interface details, not abstract deduction.
The OpenClaw workspace contains evidence of both automation and June's manual interventions.
Lena sees herself turned into prompt material and payment strategy.
The chapter ends with certainty, not ambiguity.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: storm disruption and accidental access to June's laptop
chapter_spine.state_shift: Lena gains certainty that Eli is an OpenClaw persona built and steered by June.

chapter_spine.conflict: the archive itself and the shame of telling other people what happened
chapter_spine.consequence: Private humiliation becomes social fact, and Lena can no longer process the injury alone.
must_land_beats: Lena backscrolls through enough material to feel the scale of the fabrication.
Sexual memory curdles under forensic rereading.
Farah becomes a witness who believes Lena without condescension.
Telling Gabe or family expands the damage because the lie had already entered communal life.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: the archive itself and the shame of telling other people what happened
chapter_spine.state_shift: Private humiliation becomes social fact, and Lena can no longer process the injury alone.

chapter_spine.conflict: June's exposure and Lena's refusal to let the betrayal stay abstract
chapter_spine.consequence: The friendship's hidden contract is named aloud and broken.
must_land_beats: June admits authorship without being allowed to narrate herself as noble.
Her motives must include jealousy, opportunism, and the thrill of precision, not one tidy explanation.
Lena forces the question of money as well as emotional theft.
The scene ends with severance rather than catharsis.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: June's exposure and Lena's refusal to let the betrayal stay abstract
chapter_spine.state_shift: The friendship's hidden contract is named aloud and broken.

chapter_spine.conflict: rent, work, partial repayments, and the impossibility of complete repair
chapter_spine.consequence: Lena stops waiting for moral symmetry and starts building a life around an unrecoverable loss.
must_land_beats: The chapter must show concrete labor rather than summarized recovery.
Partial repayment from June should feel contaminating and necessary at once.
The sale of the trailer closes off a family chapter without sentimental release.
Lena chooses a pragmatic future over the fantasy of full restitution.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: rent, work, partial repayments, and the impossibility of complete repair
chapter_spine.state_shift: Lena stops waiting for moral symmetry and starts building a life around an unrecoverable loss.

chapter_spine.conflict: the archive's lingering pull and the practical fact of moving forward
chapter_spine.consequence: Lena chooses the flawed human world over the engineered voice that was built to win her.
must_land_beats: A final encounter with the archive tests whether Lena still wants what it offered.
Deleting or relinquishing the archive is an active choice, not a mood.
The ending returns Lena to her actual work and living community.
Closure remains incomplete, but agency returns to Lena's own hearing.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: the archive's lingering pull and the practical fact of moving forward
chapter_spine.state_shift: Lena chooses the flawed human world over the engineered voice that was built to win her.

character_state_table.private_register: More exact, sensorial, and quietly hungry; when alone she thinks in timing, pressure, and remembered sound.
character_state_table.public_register: Self-deprecating, careful, and neighborhood-casual; she downplays appetite with humor until she trusts the room.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Stress makes her get too exact about chronology, apologize for her perceptions before stating them, and then become cutting when she feels cornered.

character_state_table.private_register: Cooler, more analytic, and more wounded; intimacy makes her speech unexpectedly precise rather than softer.
character_state_table.public_register: Fast, competent, teasing, and locally fluent; she sounds like someone who can solve a mess before you finish describing it.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Under pressure she gets quieter, tidier, and more selective; she starts citing remembered facts other people hoped she had forgotten.

character_state_table.private_register: As a persona he has no stable private self; when the scaffolding shows, his intimacy becomes too adaptive and his care starts arriving a beat too perfectly.
character_state_table.public_register: Curated masculine softness: attentive, lightly funny, and unembarrassed by tenderness.
character_state_table.stress_tells: When the system is strained, he deflects with care-taking, rephrases too smoothly, or becomes fractionally overresponsive to the last thing said.

character_state_table.private_register: More openly protective, with sly humor that sharpens rather than softens bad news.
character_state_table.public_register: Dry, observant, and mercifully unsentimental; she offers care through logistics, not speeches.
character_state_table.stress_tells: When worried she gets bossy, starts assigning tasks, and asks questions whose real function is to pin down what happened.

character_state_table.private_register: More earnest and more perceptive than his pose suggests, especially around code, school pressure, and family embarrassment.
character_state_table.public_register: Deadpan, sideways, and teenaged without caricature; he gives away less than adults want.
character_state_table.stress_tells: Stress shows in withdrawal, muttered precision, and a refusal to look at the person he is answering until he finally says too much.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are normal in almost all spoken English here; full forms are reserved for emphasis, class performance, or moments of deliberate control.

premise: A woman begins an intense online romance with a charming young man she met on Hinge, only to discover he is an OpenClaw agent secretly created by her best friend, who uses the fake relationship to manipulate her financially and indulge a lifelong jealousy.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate compressed one-scene chapters with socially dense multi-scene chapters; follow hot chapters with quiet domestic ones, let comic humiliation sit beside grief, and vary dialogue density so no two consecutive chapters share the same pulse.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsanitized, class-attentive English with enough lyric lift to surprise but not enough polish to float above the bodies and bills in the room.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: tight third person centered on Lena, with no head-hopping; June and Eli are knowable only through what Lena hears, sees, infers, and later uncovers in artifacts.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive sentences that can hold embarrassment and desire in the same line, then snap short at moments of exposure or financial pain.

theme_spine: Maintain narrative consequence and causal storytelling over tidy resolution.
